name: Havana (Cuba)

colors.symbolism: the three diagonal stripes representing the harbour and the sea
the field of the flag and the divisions between the blue stripes
elements of the city's coat of arms (gold and the three towers)
elements of the city's coat of arms

description: Three diagonal blue stripes (with white between them) running from the upper hoist to the lower fly, charged in the centre with the historic coat of arms of the City of Havana — three silver towers (Castillo de los Tres Reyes del Morro, La Punta and La Real Fuerza) and a gold key.
history: The flag of Havana, capital of Cuba, takes the three blue diagonal bands traditionally associated with the city. The arms — three towers and a key — recall Havana's historic role as the gateway to the New World: the towers represent the three colonial fortresses guarding the entrance to the harbour (El Morro, La Punta and La Real Fuerza), and the key the city's emblem as the "Llave del Nuevo Mundo" ("Key to the New World") granted by King Philip II in 1665.

features.symbolism: three blue diagonal stripes (with white between them) running from the upper hoist to the lower fly, an old emblem of the city of Havana evoking its great natural harbour

features.role: coat of arms of the City of Havana
features.symbolism: the city's historic coat of arms: three silver towers — the Castillo de los Tres Reyes del Morro, the Castillo de la Punta and the Castillo de la Real Fuerza — guarding the entrance of the harbour, and a gold key recalling Havana's title "Llave del Nuevo Mundo" ("Key to the New World"), granted by King Philip II in 1665
